title: Should not error out if you can not get container state
body:
This prevents you from cleaning up the container database, if
some how runc and friends db gets screwed up.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
I really don't like this. This is a fatal error to update state, and really ought to be treated as such...

If we could identify specific error states where runc can't find the container and convert container state to ContainerStateConfigured in those cases, that would be a lot better.

It looks like the specific runc error for no such container is `container "name" does not exist`. We can probably grep stderr of the command for "does not exist" and, if we find it, do a `c.removeConmonFiles()` and set c.state.State to ContainerStateConfigured
I guess we could ignore the error or rm and listing containers.
